2017-12-01.title: Meet North Korea’s Newest Missile

2017-12-01.title: Russia, U.S. and Other Nations Restrict Fishing in Thawing Arctic
2017-12-01.abstract: In a “landmark agreement,” eight nations and the European Union reached an agreement to impose a moratorium on fishing in newly ice-free areas in the high Arctic.

2017-12-01.title: Akihito, Emperor of Japan, Says He Will Abdicate in 2019
2017-12-01.abstract: The 83-year-old emperor will step down on April 30, 2019, becoming the first Japanese monarch to do so in two centuries.

2017-12-01.title: Europe Wanted Migrants Stopped. Now Some Are Being Sold as Slaves.
2017-12-01.abstract: A news broadcast about a market where African captives are sold drew a muted response from European officials, who are intent on keeping out migrants.

2017-12-02.abstract: Members of the nation’s new system against graft, inaugurated by the president after several scandals, say they are routinely thwarted by the government.

2017-12-02.abstract: The government ordered security forces on Friday to move against demonstrators demanding an impartial count of presidential election ballots.

2017-12-02.title: So, Meghan Markle, Are You Familiar With the Statute of Rhuddlan?
2017-12-02.abstract: Prince Harry’s fiancée will have to become familiar with that and numerous other obscure facts in order to gain British citizenship.

2017-12-25.title: Duterte’s Son Quits Post After Battling Daughter on Social Media
2017-12-25.abstract: Paolo Duterte’s daughter implied on Twitter that her father had beaten her, causing the vice mayor to strike back on Facebook.

2017-12-26.title: ‘Vulgar Butcher’ Activist in China Is Sentenced to 8 Years for Shaming Officials
2017-12-26.abstract: Under President Xi Jinping, determined activists like Wu Gan, who once survived at the edges of official tolerance, have come under concerted attack.

2017-12-26.title: 1917: Americans Skate in No Man’s Land and Bait Enemy
2017-12-26.abstract: American troops in World War I risked enemy fire to skate on a frozen canal in No Man’s Land. When the Germans began to shoot, the Americans took quick action to avoid being hit.

2017-12-26.title: North Korea Won’t Stop Its Arms Tests Anytime Soon, South Korea Warns
2017-12-26.abstract: Government and private analysts expect the North to bolster its nuclear and long-range missile capabilities in 2018 to gain leverage with Washington.

2017-12-26.title: The Last Man to Speak His Language
2017-12-26.abstract: Amadeo García García is the last man to speak Taushiro, a language spoken by an Amazon tribe. When he goes, an entire culture will disappear. We went to find him.

2017-12-26.title: Laundry Day Around the World
2017-12-26.abstract: From handwashing to laundromats, people in different parts of the world gather together to do laundry. Enter those spaces in 360 video.

2017-12-27.title: Putin May Be Re-Election Shoo-in, but He’s Taking No Chances
2017-12-27.abstract: First Aleksei A. Navalny, an anticorruption activist, was blocked from running for president. Then he was warned not to organize a boycott of the election.

2017-12-27.title: Aide Accused of Playing Role in Death of Argentine Prosecutor
2017-12-27.abstract: The charges against a computer expert employed by Alberto Nisman, who was found dead in 2015, are the first time a judge has called the case a homicide.

2017-12-27.title: Chef Gives Up a Star, Reflecting Hardship of ‘the Other France’
2017-12-27.abstract: Most chefs work a lifetime for a Michelin star. Jérôme Brochot is giving his back. Why? He can’t afford it. Neither can his declining old mining town.

2017-12-27.title: North Korean Defectors Show Signs of Possible Radiation Exposure
2017-12-27.abstract: South Korea examined the health of North Korean defectors amid fears that nuclear tests may have released radioactive fallout.

2017-12-27.title: Just Saying No: Luminaries Who Have Shunned Awards
2017-12-27.abstract: As the French chef Jérôme Brochot returns his Michelin star, here is a look at others who have turned down celebrated honors and awards.

2017-12-28.title: Once a Cash Cow, Venezuela’s Oil Company Now Verges on Collapse
2017-12-28.abstract: The deepening troubles threaten to further destabilize a nation facing a dire recession and unbridled crime, as well as food and medicine shortages.

2017-12-28.title: At an Air Show in China, Drones, Not Jets, Are the Stars
2017-12-28.abstract: China makes the world’s most popular drones. The country’s passion for unmanned flying machines runs deep — and starts at an early age.

2017-12-28.title: Tibetan Filmmaker Flees to U.S. After ‘Arduous’ Escape from China
2017-12-28.abstract: The filmmaker was jailed for interviewing Tibetans about their lives under Chinese rule. His escape reflects a growing climate of repression in China.

2017-12-28.title: Islamic State Claims Deadly Blast at Afghan Shiite Center
2017-12-28.abstract: A suicide bomber struck a crowded gathering in Kabul, leaving more than 40 dead. The group has claimed several attacks on Shiite targets across the country.

2017-12-28.title: After St. Petersburg Explosion, Putin Orders Police to ‘Liquidate’ Terrorists
2017-12-28.abstract: Vladimir V. Putin, who is running for a fourth term as Russia’s president, has burnished his reputation for being tough on extremists.

2017-12-29.title: Iran and Saudis’ Latest Power Struggle: Expanding Rights for Women
2017-12-29.abstract: The archrivals of the Middle East appear to be vying over who can be quicker to overhaul their repressive rules for women.

2017-12-29.title: South Korea Seizes Ship Suspected of Sending Oil to North Korea
2017-12-29.abstract: The seizure of the Lighthouse Winmore was revealed after President Trump accused China of secretly transferring petroleum to North Korean ships.

2017-12-29.title: Mumbai Restaurant Fire Kills at Least 14
2017-12-29.abstract: A blaze engulfed two rooftop restaurants in Mumbai on Friday. The police filed criminal complaints against restaurant managers.

2017-12-29.title: How Did Bombs Made in Italy Kill a Family In Yemen?
2017-12-29.abstract: We followed shipments of bombs from a holiday island in Italy to Saudi Arabia, then found those bombs at the scene of civilian deaths in Yemen. Is Italy capitalizing on a brutal conflict or just doing business?

2017-12-29.title: Gunmen Attack Cairo Church, Killing at Least 9
2017-12-29.abstract: The Islamic State claimed responsibility for the assault on a Coptic Orthodox Church, which was the latest in a wave of violence targeting the Christian minority in Egypt.

2017-12-30.title: City of the Future? Humans, Not Technology, Are the Challenge in Toronto
2017-12-30.abstract: Plans by a Google sibling for a development where robots collect trash and heated paths melt snow have generated excitement. But its data-collection sensors have spurred privacy concerns.

2017-12-30.title: 20 Great Reads You Probably Missed in 2017
2017-12-30.abstract: With news breaking at a relentless pace, you might have missed some terrific articles The New York Times published this year. Here’s a selection.

2017-12-30.title: Pandas, U.F.O.s and Other Curiosities from Britain’s National Archives
2017-12-30.abstract: British government memos, tweets and other documents find their way to the National Archives, with some released each year. Here’s a quirky look at Friday’s yield.

2017-12-30.title: The BBC in Pidgin? People Like It Well-Well
2017-12-30.abstract: The British broadcaster, once a bastion of “received pronunciation,” has learned that if it wants to reach new audiences, it needs to try new approaches.

2017-12-30.title: Some Afghan Children Find an Alternative to Jail — for Now
2017-12-30.abstract: Orphanages for Afghan children of jailed mothers meet part of the need, but funding cuts threaten their future and many others stay in prison.

2017-12-31.title: Congo Reports Arrest in Killing of 2 U.N. Experts
2017-12-31.abstract: The experts, Michael Sharp, an American, and Zaida Catalan, a Swede, were investigating crimes associated with a rebellion in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

2017-12-31.title: Greek Prime Minister Seeks to Block Asylum for Turkish Officer
2017-12-31.abstract: In an apparent effort to avoid antagonizing Turkey, Greece’s prime minister moved to block asylum for a Turkish officer who fled last year’s failed coup.

2017-12-31.title: Rouhani Urges Calm in Iran as Protests Continue
2017-12-31.abstract: Two demonstrators were reported killed in recent days as protests quickly grew into a nationwide outpouring of anger.

2017-12-31.title: A Year in Bloopers: The Making of The Daily 360
2017-12-31.abstract: Happy New Year from the Daily 360 team! We've made 435 videos in 426 days. Here are some of the moments that didn’t make the cut. Pro tip: If you can see the camera, the camera can see you.

2017-12-31.title: Laughter, Then Horror, as Flames Engulfed Upscale Mumbai Restaurants
2017-12-31.abstract: The fire at two restaurants on Friday killed 14 people. Patrons said the establishments had no fire alarms, sprinklers or clear emergency exits.
